data.title: a16z: From retail traders to top institutions, predicting the market is maturing.
data.description:
Authors: Alex Immerman, Santiago Rodriguez, Partners at a16z Growth Fund; Source: a16z; Compiled by: Shaw Jinse Finance

The financial sector is highly segmented, with each segment boasting its own recognized top-tier benchmark conferences. Leaders in healthcare services, payers, and biotechnology gather annually in San Francisco for the JPMorgan Healthcare Conference. Global macroeconomic giants and politicians travel to the Swiss Alps each year for the Davos Forum. Technology, media, telecommunications, real estate, industry, financial services, and virtually every other sector have their own signature summits.

In late March, Kalshi Research, Kalshi's division focusing on academic and institutional research, held its first research conference in New York, bringing together academic experts, Wall Street executives, former politicians, and traders who influence market trends. The composition of the attendees perfectly illustrates that "this industry is maturing." The conference opened with a dialogue between Kalshi co-founders Tarek Mansour and Luana Lopes Lara and Bloomberg reporter Katherine Doherty. Below are some key viewpoints on industry development from this dialogue and subsequent roundtable discussions.

1. Life (and Markets) is More Than Just Elections and Sports

Major news cycles often exhibit a pattern: a hot event (such as the 2024 election, the Super Bowl, or the recent NCAA March Madness basketball tournament) dominates headlines, driving up prediction market trading volume and creating the illusion that "prediction markets can only do that."

However, although early views generally held that prediction markets were only viable during election cycles, Kalshi...

... Significant growth has already been achieved in other areas. During this research conference, weekly sports transactions approached $3 billion, accounting for approximately 80% of Kalshi's total transactions, primarily driven by the NCAA March Madness basketball tournament. Tarek Mansour and Luana Lopes Lara define the dominance of the sports category as a temporary phenomenon. More relevant data shows that even though the absolute transaction volume of sports transactions reached a record high, its share of total transaction volume is actually at a historical low. All other categories are growing faster. They point out that entertainment, cryptocurrency, and politics & culture are showing stronger user growth and better transaction retention than sports. Sports act as a catalyst for the mass market, serving as a familiar, regularly scheduled, and emotionally driven product for attracting users. However, the company also achieved significant growth in the long tail market, which accounts for more than 20% of Kalshi's remaining trading volume and is crucial for institutional hedging and the information market. A subsequent institutional panel discussion corroborated this view from the demand side: Cyril Goddeeris, co-head of global equities at Goldman Sachs, stated that forecasts related to macroeconomic events and CPI data are the most closely watched areas on Wall Street. Sally Shin, executive vice president of growth at CNBC, said she has been using forecasts related to the Federal Reserve Chairman's market and non-farm payroll data as a narrative tool. Troy Dixon, co-head of global markets at Tradeweb, envisions a future where large Wall Street banks will establish dedicated prediction market trading departments, with financial contracts as their core product. 2. Why Kalshi Gains Wall Street's Attention Traditional financial markets function for many reasons, a key one being the existence of universally accepted benchmarks for each major asset class: the S&P 500 is the average performance of 500 stocks; crude oil has a benchmark price set by the Intercontinental Exchange (ICE). However, **in terms of political and economic events** (such as who wins an election, whether a tariff bill passes, or the outcome of a Supreme Court case), **previously there were almost no widely accepted and dynamically updated benchmarks. Prediction markets have changed this.** Today, the future trajectory of almost any event has a real-time, liquid market benchmark. Once a credible price is available for an event, such as a quote indicating the probability of a 30% tariff bill passing, institutional counterparties can trade at that price. This creates a mechanism for directly trading events or hedging downside risk in other parts of a portfolio. As Tradeweb's Troy Dixon stated: "Recall when Trump was first elected; there was a lot of hedging activity in the stock market. The strategy was to short the S&P 500 because it was widely believed that a Trump victory would cause the stock market to fall. This trade resulted in huge losses. The core problem is: how do you price these kinds of events? What is the benchmark?" Tarek Mansour also mentioned a similar motivation for founding Kalshi: he previously worked in Goldman Sachs' trading department, where the team's recommended trades were linked to the 2024 US election and Brexit. Without market prediction, institutions attempting to hedge political or macroeconomic event risks with related assets are essentially placing two bets simultaneously: one on the outcome of the event itself, and the other on the correlation between the event and the traded asset. The second bet could potentially fail independently. With a direct benchmark for the event itself, these two bets become one. As Tarek Mansour says, "This market group is now pricing in all kinds of events." 3. Three Stages of Large-Scale Institutional Adoption It's premature to say that large Wall Street institutions are already trading large amounts on Kalshi: currently, institutions mostly use Kalshi as a data source rather than a trading platform. However, Luana Lopes Lara states that the path for Wall Street to more broadly access this market is very clear, summarizing it in three stages: Stage One: Data Application Integrating price data into institutional workflows, until Goldman Sachs portfolio managers habitually browse Kalshi's probability quotes like they would the VIX volatility index, has become a reality to some extent. As Johns Hopkins University professor and former Federal Reserve official Jonathan Wright stated, "Kalshi is almost a unique source of reference for data such as Fed decisions, unemployment rates, and GDP." **Phase Two: System Integration** Completing compliance and legal approvals, technical system integration, and internal staff training—the entire process of introducing a new financial instrument. **Phase Three: Practical Implementation** Actual risk hedging on exchanges, with trading volume and market depth beginning a positive cycle. At this stage, more hedging positions attract more speculative positions, narrower bid-ask spreads attract more hedgers, and the market benchmark becomes self-reinforcing. Currently, most institutions are still in Phase 1, a significant number have entered Phase 2, and only a few have reached Phase 3. A major reason why many institutions have failed to enter Phase 3 is that current market prediction contracts require the full notional value as margin – a $100 position requires $100 to be deposited with the clearinghouse. This is acceptable for retail traders, but a significant limitation for hedge funds or banks that rely on leverage and return on capital. Tarek Mansour stated, “If you want to hedge $100, you have to deposit $100 with a clearinghouse. That’s too expensive for institutions. Companies like Citadel Investments and Millennium Management wouldn’t do that.” Kalshi recently obtained a license from the National Futures Association (NFA) and is working with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to push for margin trading. 4. Future Direction Bloomberg’s Head of Market Innovation, Michael McDonough, put it most bluntly: “Success means these products become commonplace.” He likened it to the options market in the 1970s: when concerns about market manipulation and regulatory uncertainty were similar, these issues were eventually resolved as infrastructure improved, becoming so routine that no one paid much attention anymore. Toby Moskowitz, a partner at AQR Management, said he is “betting real money” that the market will become a mature tool available to institutions within five years—or even sooner. Garrett Herren of the voting platform Vote Hub described the final state of maturity: “The question of the future will no longer be whether we should use prediction markets, but how we should use them. Once people start asking this question, it means it has become indispensable.” In fact, although prediction markets are currently small, the hedging market itself is extremely large: [Image of prediction markets] In fact, the normalization of prediction markets has already begun. During the political panel discussion, former Congressman Mondaire Jones pointed out that top figures in both parties' Congress—including President Trump, House Minority Leader Jeffries, and Senate Minority Leader Schumer—have publicly cited Kalshi's probability data. DDHQ's Scott Tranter confirmed that prediction market data has now become a standard reference indicator within party committees. Vote Hub also announced that it will directly integrate Kalshi data into its midterm election prediction model. All of this was unimaginable two years ago. Two years ago, the most successful traders on Kalshi were just a group of amateurs. Now the situation is different, and calling them "amateurs" is no longer appropriate. In Kalshi's panel discussion, "Traders Behind the Markets," four traders shared their experiences making a living from this field, their professional habits mirroring those of professional traders—one having dedicated eleven years to studying Billboard data, and another having entered the prediction market as early as 2006, when it was merely a "nerdy hobby" without real financial involvement. None of the four panelists had a financial background; they came from the fields of music, politics, and poker, respectively, but they unanimously agreed that the platform truly rewards deep expertise in the field, not impressive credentials. Prediction markets have come a long way. Initially a novel concept in academia, they became a hot topic during elections, and later a derivative of sports betting. This conference clearly demonstrated that prediction markets are maturing into an infrastructure providing pricing for uncertainty, serving participants from retail traders to top institutions, with increasingly diverse applications.

data.title: Anthropic's latest Opus 4.7 model features 8 Hidden Blades.
data.description:
Author: Alan Walker, Silicon Valley

The press conference shone the spotlight on the SWE-bench, but the real signals were hidden in the footnotes, the introductory blocks, and an inconspicuous phrase like "auto mode."

On California Ave in Palo Alto, the light of 9:30 in the morning swept obliquely through the glass windows of Coupa Café, illuminating Alan Walker's half-cold flat white. He had just finished browsing Anthropic's website, leaned back in his chair, and began to speak to Tony, who had just sat down opposite him.

"Anthropic's Opus 4.7 launch event was quite restrained—the main focus was on the SWE-bench pillars, the customer quote slideshow, and a beautiful alignment diagram. Most tech media simply copied the press release and left." "But the real substance is buried in the footnotes, the migration guide, and a casual mention of 'auto mode extended to Max users.' You have to read it like a 10-K report—the main text is for retail investors, the footnotes are for institutions." "Before I finish this cup of coffee today, I'll disassemble eight knives. Each one will tell you who it's going to cut." (Image of a 7453650 image) "xhigh is not a gear upgrade — Default has been secretly increased." (Image of a 7453650 image) The press conference briefly mentioned: "In Claude Code, we've raised the default effort level to xhigh for all plans." Most people, seeing "xhigh," assume it means "another level has been added," like adding a new color to an iPhone. Wrong. The real signal is in the last half of the sentence—the default level for all plans in Claude Code has been raised to xhigh. This is a very Anthropic move: quietly raising everyone's baseline by one level, while keeping the computing power bill unchanged. It's like giving you a smarter colleague, but without a raise.

TONY: Wait, doesn't this mean that Pro users who used to spend $20 for medium can now directly get xhigh?

ALAN: Yes. And read that Hex quote carefully—"low-effort 4.7 ≈ medium-effort 4.6". With the default tier boosted, the effective intelligence that ordinary users get jumps two whole tiers. The press conference didn't capitalize this number because they didn't want the token consumption page to look bad. ◆ Application Scenarios On Monday morning, you ask Claude Code to modify a 500-line backend module—before, you had to manually type `/effort max` before you dared to let it run automatically; now you don't need to configure anything, it defaults to xhigh, and you can finish the work after a cup of coffee. This difference isn't just 10% faster, it's "you don't need to worry about it anymore."

 KILL LIST

→ "AI Tuning / prompt configuration" type SaaS——Those tools that teach you how to adjust thinking budget and how to select effort, the default values ​​are automatically correct, the middle layer has no business

→ Junior Engineer Position——xhigh's default work is already the quality lower limit of a three-year experienced engineer

→ Outsourced Code Review Company——The third knife below will kill this

——BLADE NO. 02

Auto Mode —— Permission UI The Silent Revolution

The third line of the press conference footnote: "Auto mode extended to Max users." Just one sentence.

Anthropic's official website quote: "auto mode is a new permissions option where Claude makes decisions on your behalf."——"Make decisions on your behalf."

In the past year, all agent startups have been caught between two extremes: either skip-all-permissions (the Devin, Cognition path), or bombarding users with approval/deny pop-ups (early Cursor). Anthropic took a third path: training the model to judge for itself what should and shouldn't be asked, and internalizing this judgment into auto mode.

KAI: Alan, what's the fundamental difference between this and skip permissions? Aren't they both just letting it run?

ALAN: The difference is huge. Skip is like pulling the safety plug; you're responsible if something goes wrong. Auto is like the model installing its own safety mechanism—it proactively stops and asks you for help with dangerous operations, and handles low-risk operations itself. Essentially, it moves the entire "permission UI" layer from the product's outer shell to the model's weights. TONY: So those YC startups making "agent governance/guardrails"... ALAN: The product is essentially built into the model. This is a perfect example of what Andrej said last year: "the model is the product."

KILL LIST

→Agent guardrails / approval-flow SaaS——Those who make "human-machine collaborative approval platforms" have had their entire category downgraded

→RPA Traditional Industries (UiPath / Automation Anywhere)——Their core value is "controllable automation," and now controllability is inherent

→BPO Outsourcing Industry Back-office Operations——Data entry, customer service assignment, invoice reconciliation in the Philippines and India, running in auto mode for a whole day is the work of an entire team

——BLADE NO. 03

/ultrareview—An Assassination Order for Senior Engineers

Official wording: "a dedicated review session that reads through changes and flags bugs and design issues that a careful reviewer would catch."

Note that word—"a careful reviewer." Not junior, not linter, but "careful reviewer." In layman's terms: senior engineer.

CodeRabbit's David Loker provides even more direct figures: recall increased by over 10%, uncovering the most difficult bugs in the most complex PRs, with precision almost not dropping. Increased recall and undiminished precision—in the code review field, this is the holy grail. The last person to achieve this combination was a Google internal Tricorder, who worked on it for ten years.

MARCUS: At FAANG, a staff member earns $800K a year, and half of that time is spent reviewing PRs. If this thing really works…

ALAN: Pro and Max users get three free UltraReviews, letting you try it out. This is a common Silicon Valley tactic of "freemium poisoning"—giving you a taste, then making you unable to go back. MARCUS: So this isn't a tool, it's a substitute. ALAN: Not entirely. It doesn't replace staff, it replaces the two hours staff spend reviewing ten PRs every afternoon. Those freed-up two hours are for senior engineers, not human GitHub bots. ◆ Application Scenarios: A 20-person engineering team where the tech lead used to spend three hours a day reviewing PRs. On UltraReview, the tech lead only needs to look at the "design issues" highlighted by Claude—three hours reduced to twenty minutes, the saved time actually used for architecture. This isn't "AI assistance," it's a rewriting of job responsibilities.

KILL LIST

→ All independent AI code review startups — CodeRabbit, Codacy, Qodo, are now features of Anthropic

→ Traditional security scanning tools (Snyk/Checkmarx) — Rule-driven static scanning is crushed by the "read code like a human" approach

→ Outsourced code review services in India/Eastern Europe — This market, valued at billions of dollars over the past decade, has now evaporated

—BLADE NO. 04

"The longest side of the acceptable image is now 2,576 pixels, approximately 3.75 megapixels, more than three times the previous size."

This is the most underestimated point. Most people see it and think, "Oh, it's higher resolution." That's a huge mistake. This is the watershed moment for the entire computer-use category, marking its transition from demo to production.

The evidence is in the quote at the very bottom of the release page, a quote from XBOW CEO Oege de Moor—54.5% → 98.5%. This isn't a gradual improvement; it's a leap from "unusable" to "unavoidable." Opus 4.6 was still guessing where the buttons on the screen were, while 4.7 could already read small print and nested tables on dense dashboards.

SARAH: Our enterprise clients have been stuck at this point. 4.6 When we tried to automate the processing of scanned invoices, half of them were wrong—the boss simply said, "Stop messing around."

ALAN: Now, the 98.5% accuracy rate means that RPA, IT operations, expense auditing, legacy system migration—all workflows that still rely on human visual input on screens—have, for the first time, an acceptable baseline model.

KAI: Computer use is no longer a demo video; it's productivity.

ALAN: Yes, and note—this is a model-level upgrade, not API parameters. Existing users don't need to change anything; they automatically benefit from it. Anthropic is quietly pushing the product capabilities of all integrators to a higher level.

KILL LIST

→ OCR / Document Understanding SaaS (Rossum / Hyperscience / Nanonets) —— Their moat was originally "vision + structured," but now it's being caught up with or even surpassed by general models

→ The traditional RPA giants —— UiPath's screen recognition core technology, its value evaporated by half overnight

→ Enterprise application data entry departments —— Medical insurance claims, bank KYC, government form processing, the whole process is a human assembly line

→ Independent penetration testing / red team industry —— Companies like XBOW actually benefited, but traditional Pentesting Consulting Services Exposed

—— BLADE NO. 05

File-System Memory——Anthropic Chooses the Simplest Path

A footnote at the press conference: "Opus 4.7 is better at using file system-based memory. It remembers important notes across long, multi-session work."

OpenAI is using "embedded memory"—memory embedded in the model, invisible and unchangeable. Google is working on the mysterious infini-attention. Anthropic has revealed its hand this time: 'The file system is memory.' Claude writes and reads .md notes, which you can then easily parse and view. This choice may seem low-tech, but it's actually a victory of first principles. The core issue of memory is never storage, but auditability, editability, and transferability. Vector databases and embedded memory both violate these three principles.

ERIC: The biggest fear for enterprise customers is, "What exactly has this AI remembered about me? I don't know."

ALAN: File system memory directly solves compliance. GDPR erasure rights? Just rm it. SOC2 audit? CAT it to the auditor. This isn't a technological advantage, it's a legal advantage.

ERIC:So those startups working on "AI memory layers"...

ALAN:Mem0, LangMem, Zep — raised a lot of money this year. They solved the problem of "models not managing their own memory." Anthropic has written this capability into the model, and uses the most basic POSIX file system. The intermediate layer is skipped.

KILL LIST

→ AI Memory Infrastructure Startups (Mem0 / LangMem / Zep) — Value Proposition Internalized into Models

→ Agentic Memory Use Cases for Some Vector Databases — A Major Narrative of Pinecone and Weaviate Affected

→ AI Enhancement Layer for Enterprise Knowledge Management SaaS — No More Third-Party Middleware Needed, Claude Directly Reads and Writes Project Files

—BLADE NO. 06

Task Budgets — For Agents

Install the brakes, then release the accelerator. "Giving developers a way to guide Claude's token spend so it can prioritize work across longer runs." (public beta) This was overlooked by all the media, but it's the most important engineering breakthrough for long-running agents this year. Over the past year, all agent companies have been struggling with the same demon: long-running tokens are out of control. Give Devin or Cursor a complex task, it runs for two hours, and comes back telling you it burned through $800, only half the work is done. The boss's eyes turn green when he sees the bill. The task budget design is ingenious—it's not a simple token cap, but rather, it allows the model to see the budget countdown and decide which steps to skip and how to achieve the most critical level of completion. This is exactly the "minimum deliverable" mindset in project management. Anthropic has trained the PM skill of scope-cutting into its model. Given a $10 budget to run the agent, it will automatically decide which features to complete at 80% and which must be 100%. TONY: Therefore, Notion's quote—"implicit-need tests"—is correct. ALAN: The model is starting to have "resource awareness," able to guess what you expect but haven't explicitly stated, and prioritize it within the budget. This is training "senior engineer judgment."

KILL LIST

→ AI cost-control / LLM observable startup (Helicone / Langfuse cost module)——Core functionality is nativeized

→ Agent orchestration framework (partial LangGraph / CrewAI usage)——The model can plan the budget itself, without external scheduling

→ Project management part of the traditional consulting industry——The intelligence of "resource allocation + delivery tailoring" is handled by the model

—— BLADE NO. 07

Joe Haddad, Distinguished Eng at Vercel: "It even does proofs on systems code before starting work, which is new behavior we haven't seen from earlier Claude models."

This sentence is buried among more than twenty quotes, and no one has highlighted it. But the old OG put down his coffee as soon as he read this.

"proofs on systems code"—before writing system-level code, the model will first perform mathematical/formal proofs on its own. This doesn't mean it's smarter; it means the model is starting to verify its own code using the same methods as PhD students verifying their dissertations ...>

Joe Haddad, Distinguished Eng at Vercel: "It even does proofs on systems code before starting work, which is new behavior we haven't seen from earlier Claude models."

This sentence is buried among more than twenty quotes, and no one has highlighted it. But the old OG put down his coffee as soon as he read this.

MARCUS: This behavior appears in the training data, indicating that Anthropic explicitly rewards "proof before coding" during the RL phase.

ALAN: Yes, this was consciously trained. Combining Vercel's section on "loop resistance" with Genspark's, and Hex's "correctly reports when data is missing instead of plausible-but-incorrect fallbacks"—what you see is a complete, tasteful training engineering approach: making the model work like an engineer who isn't easily fooled. MARCUS: Not easily fooled—meaning not deceiving itself. ALAN: Yes. Opus 4.7 no longer just provides a seemingly workable solution to complete a task. This is a true embodiment of alignment at the product level.

KILL LIST

→Formal Verification Tool Sub-market (Partial)—Some introductory scenarios for high-barrier tools like Coq/Lean/TLA+, where the model handles it for you

→High-Frequency Trading / Blockchain Security Audit Industry—The core work of auditors ("reading code to find invariant violations") is collaboratively handled by the model, reducing audit costs

→Operating System Kernel / Embedded System Outsourcing—Those sub-sectors requiring proof-based reasoning have had their barriers to entry lowered

— BLADE NO. 08

Cyber ​​Verification—The Window for Regulatory Arbitrage Has Been Opened

"During its training, we experimented with efforts to differentially reduce these capabilities."

The most ingenious move is here. Anthropic admits that during training, they **actively reduced the network attack and defense capabilities of Opus 4.7** because the more powerful Mythos Preview behind it wasn't released. Then—

Then they launched a **Cyber ​​Verification Program**, allowing legitimate security researchers, pentesters, and red team members to unlock higher privileges after certification.

ERIC: This…isn't this just a model version of export controls?

ALAN: More accurately, it's "Capability KYC". The model has three levels of capability gates; you need to prove your identity to unlock the corresponding level. For the first time, the window for regulatory arbitrage is being openly priced by AI companies themselves. What does this mean for startups? First, for general "AI + Security" startups aiming for high-end applications, Anthropic certification is essential, effectively controlling the supply chain. Second, a completely new category will emerge: consulting services that help you achieve Anthropic certification—similar to companies that help you pass SOC2 certification today. Third, this is Anthropic practicing how to release all future frontier models; Mythos's release will only be more stringent. Therefore, companies like Palantir and Booz Allen, with their strong government compliance backgrounds… have gained an extra layer of competitive advantage. They already possessed liquidation-level status, now naturally unlocking the top-level model. ◆ Application Scenarios A Y Combinator entrepreneur wanting to do AI pentesting will, starting in Q2 2026, have to answer on the first page of their business plan, "Have you obtained the Anthropic Cyber ​​Verification?" No? VCs won't invest. Obtained? Valuation doubled. A certification, a watershed moment in the capital market.

KILL LIST & New Track

→General Cybersecurity Startup SaaS—Without Anthropic certification, you can't get upper-level model capabilities, and your ceiling is locked

→The Emergence of a New Track: "AI Model Capability Compliance Consulting"—A Batch of Intermediaries Helping Enterprises with Frontier Model Certification Will Emerge in the Next 12 Months

→Traditional Military and Government Integrators (Palantir / Booz Allen)—Naturally Benefit, Barriers Become Moats

→Open Source / The local deployment camp—Llama, Qwen, and DeepSeek—benefited, with "usable without authentication" becoming a core selling point. Outside the window, the sun on California Ave had already climbed over the roof of Palo Alto Creamery, its rays slanting across the glass. "Eight knives, slashing in eight directions. Some tracks die today, some begin to live." "The real stuff about each generation of frontier models isn't written in the headline," he told Tony. "The launch event is for analysts. The numbers in the footnotes and quotes are what we see." "Don't just watch the show."

data.title: Charles Schwab launches cryptocurrency spot trading; Anthropic releases latest model.
data.description:
Headline

▌Charles Schwab Announces Launch of Spot Crypto Trading Service

According to the Wall Street Journal, Charles Schwab officially announced the launch of its Schwab Crypto spot cryptocurrency trading service on April 16, which will be rolled out to retail clients in phases over the next few weeks. The service supports direct trading of Bitcoin and Ethereum, and includes educational content and professional customer support, while integrating Charles Schwab's existing investment, trading, wealth management, and banking services.

▌Anthropic Releases its Latest Model OPUS 4.7

On April 16, Anthropic released its latest model, Claude Opus 4.7, which is now fully available.

▌JPMorgan Chase: Clarity Act Negotiations Enter Final Stage, Disputes Reduced to 2-3 Core Issues

On April 16, JPMorgan Chase analysts stated that negotiations on the U.S. Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act (Clarity Act) have entered the final stage, with both sides reaching compromises on the remaining few points of contention. The disputes have been reduced from over a dozen to 2-3 core issues, with discussions on stablecoin rewards "in good shape." While banks have expressed concerns about stablecoins offering deposit-like returns, the overall trend is one of bipartisan compromise. JPMorgan Chase believes that "no bill is perfect," and if passed, the act will provide significant regulatory clarity for the integration of digital assets into the U.S. financial system. The remaining major disagreements focus on stablecoin rewards, DeFi regulation, and token classification. Although optimism is rising, the bill still faces the risk of delay due to the 2026 midterm elections, potentially entering a more uncertain political environment.

... If the bill is ultimately passed, it will divide the regulatory powers of the SEC and CFTC, providing a long-term regulatory framework for stablecoins, DeFi, and the entire crypto industry.

▌Trump says next US-Iran meeting may be held this weekend

On April 17, US President Trump, speaking about the Iran issue on April 16 local time, said he was unsure whether the ceasefire needed to be extended, but that significant progress was being made. Trump also stated that the next meeting with Iran might be held this weekend. (CCTV News)

▌Trump says he may travel to Pakistan to push for an agreement with Iran

On April 17, US President Trump stated that he might travel to Pakistan to push for an agreement with Iran.

He told reporters at the White House, "If the agreement is signed in Islamabad, I might go. The (Pakistani) Field Marshal did a very good job, and the Prime Minister did a very good job. So I might go. They want me to go." Trump had previously stated that the US might hold talks with Iran this weekend, saying, "We are very close to reaching an agreement." On April 17, local time on April 16, US President Trump stated that the US had received a "very substantial" statement to ensure that Iran would not possess nuclear weapons, and that this commitment would be valid for more than 20 years. However, Trump did not disclose the details of the so-called "statement." Trump also stated that Iran had agreed not to possess nuclear weapons, and that the prospects for reaching an agreement "look very optimistic." He said, "Iran has agreed to almost all of the demands." Currently, there has been no response from Iran. (CCTV News) Trump: Lebanon and Israel Agree to Ceasefire On April 16, US President Trump posted that he had just had a very pleasant meeting with the highly respected President of Lebanon and Prime Minister of Israel, Netanyahu. The two leaders have agreed to formally begin a 10-day ceasefire at 5 p.m. Eastern Time in order to achieve peace between the two countries. On Tuesday, representatives of both countries met with our great Secretary of State, Rubio, in Washington, D.C., for the first time in 34 years. He instructed Vice President Vance, Secretary of State Rubio, and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Kaine to work with Israel and Lebanon to achieve lasting peace. It has been a great honor to resolve nine wars around the world, and this will be the tenth, so let's get this done!

▌Israeli Media: Israel Agrees to 10-Day Ceasefire but Will Not Withdraw Troops from Lebanon

On April 17, according to multiple Israeli media reports on the 16th, following a request from US President Trump, Israel has agreed to a 10-day ceasefire with Hezbollah in Lebanon, but will not withdraw its troops from southern Lebanon. Israeli public broadcaster and Channel 12 reported that Prime Minister Netanyahu convened a telephone conference with his security cabinet that day, informing them that he had agreed to Trump's ceasefire request. The reports said that Netanyahu stated during the meeting that Israeli troops will maintain their existing deployment in southern Lebanon. Israel considers this area a security "buffer zone" and stated that the current ceasefire agreement's terms allow the Israeli military to continue taking action upon detecting a "threat."

(Xinhua News Agency) Blockchain Applications ▌ETH Foundation Core Member Josh Stark Announces Resignation On April 17, Josh Stark, a core figure at the Ethereum Foundation (EF), announced his resignation after five years in office. He will officially step down at the end of the month. Stark joined the Ethereum Foundation in 2019, initially working on a special projects team before rising to leadership positions. He worked with EF Chairman Aya Miyaguchi, Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin, and co-executive directors Hsiao-Wei Wang and Bastian Aue, among other senior figures. He was one of the foundation's most prominent members, leading several major developments in Ethereum, including "The Merge," the upgrade from Proof-of-Work to Proof-of-Stake, and subsequent upgrades such as Dencun, Fusaka, and Pectra. During last year's leadership reshuffle at the Ethereum Foundation, he was appointed Co-Manager of the EF Board of Directors and recently became Co-Chair of the "Trillion Dollar Security" initiative. Last month, Stark co-authored an Ethereum strategy blog post with Josh Rudolf and Julian Ma, outlining Ethereum's latest scaling direction and its relationship with the Layer 2 ecosystem. Stark stated, "I haven't planned for the future yet; I just want to rest and spend time with family and friends." This departure comes against the backdrop of ongoing changes at the Ethereum Foundation. Last year, the foundation underwent a major leadership reshuffle, subsequently refocusing its strategic emphasis on Ethereum mainnet scaling and core cypherpunk values. Tomasz K. Stańczak also resigned as Co-Executive Director at the end of February this year, after serving less than a year. On the same day, Trent Van Epps also announced his departure from EF to dedicate himself full-time to Protocol Guild, the independent funding organization for Ethereum core developers that he founded.

▌Coinbase: Gold and Silver Futures to Trade 24/7

On April 17, Coinbase announced, "Gold and silver futures will trade 24/7. Starting May 1, these markets will be the first in U.S. history to offer 24/7 trading. As the world's oldest store of value, they will now have modern trading hours."

▌OpenAI Launches Drug Discovery AI Model to Challenge Google

On April 17, OpenAI launched an early version of an artificial intelligence (AI) model designed to accelerate drug discovery, joining tech companies in a race to prove that AI can drive more scientific breakthroughs. The ChatGPT developer said Thursday that the model, named GPT-Rosalind, is geared towards life science research, such as helping to extract insights from massive amounts of data and translating research into patient-facing medical applications.

... OpenAI stated that the model will initially be available to select enterprise clients in a research preview format. Initial users include pharmaceutical company Amgen, vaccine manufacturer Moderna, and the Allen Institute, a non-profit bioscience research organization. OpenAI, Anthropic, and Alphabet's Google are increasingly focusing on the application of AI in science and medicine, including assisting in new drug development and analyzing personal medical data. Cryptocurrency ▌US Government Transfers 8.2 BTC to Coinbase Prime On April 16, according to Arkham monitoring, the US government (funds seized after the Bitfinex hack) deposited 8.2 BTC (approximately $606,000) into Coinbase Prime.

▌Drift: Receives $147.5 Million in Funding from Tether and Others for User Recovery

On April 16th, Drift announced that the Drift Protocol has received support from Tether and other partners, with Tether contributing $127.5 million and other partners contributing $20 million, to jointly support user recovery efforts following the April 1st attack. This support package includes a $100 million revenue-linked credit line, ecosystem grants, and loans to market makers. Drift will establish a dedicated user recovery pool, aiming to gradually resolve the $295 million in outstanding user losses as trading revenue grows. In addition, Drift will issue independent recovery tokens to affected users, representing a claim to the recovery pool and are transferable. Currently, Drift is undergoing a protocol reboot, has engaged Ottersec and Asymmetric for audits, and is migrating its settlement layer from USDC to USDT.

The previous attack resulted in the theft of assets worth approximately $295 million, but the insurance fund's assets were unaffected. On April 16th, according to Lookonchain monitoring, Abraxas Capital (Alpha Bitcoin Fund) deposited 1,993 bitcoins into Kraken, worth $148.32 million. Since March 14th, Abraxas Capital (Alpha Bitcoin Fund) has cumulatively deposited 9,582 bitcoins into Kraken, worth $691 million, and currently still holds 20,337 bitcoins, worth $1.51 billion. BlackRock Transfers 3,446 Bitcoins from Coinbase, Worth Approximately $255.2 Million On April 16th, according to Onchain Lens monitoring, BlackRock transferred 3,446 Bitcoins from Coinbase yesterday, worth approximately $255.2 million. Arthur Hayes: BTC Won't See Significant Rise Until Fed Provides Liquidity to Fill Bank Balance Sheet Gaps On April 16th, according to Cointelegraph, Arthur Hayes stated that Bitcoin (BTC) will not see a substantial rise until the Federal Reserve provides liquidity to fill the gaps in bank balance sheets. He currently holds an unleveraged long position in BTC and is bullish on HYPE amid uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz.

▌Tom Lee: BTC, ETH, and MAG7 Stocks Expected to Lead the Next Bull Market

On April 16th, according to Cointelegraph, Fundstrat's Tom Lee stated that despite the S&P 500 hitting a record high, investors remain skeptical. However, he expects Bitcoin, Ethereum, and MAG7 stocks to lead the next bull market.

▌Binance to Launch Three US Stock USDT-Margined Perpetual Contracts: Microsoft, Broadcom, and Alibaba

On April 16th, Binance will launch three US stock USDT-margined perpetual contracts: MSFTUSDT (Microsoft), AVGOUSDT (Broadcom), and BABAUSDT (Alibaba).

**Important Economic Developments** **▌US Launches Operation Codenamed "Economic Fury" Against Iran** **April 16th News:** On April 16th local time, US Defense Secretary Hergsays stated at a press conference that the US Treasury Department is launching an operation codenamed "Economic Fury" to "exert maximum economic pressure on Iran." (CCTV) **▌Torrent Fees for the Strait of Hormuz to be Paid Through Iranian Banks** **April 16th News:** According to the Iranian Students' News Agency, parliamentary officials stated that Iran plans to pay tolls for passage through the Strait of Hormuz through Iranian banks.

▌US Banking Committee Democrats: Warsh's Hearing Should Be Postponed

On April 16, Democratic members of the US Senate Banking Committee stated that the hearing for Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Kevin Warsh should be postponed.

▌Federal Reserve's Williams: Now Is Not the Time to Give Guidance on Interest Rates

On April 16, Federal Reserve Chairman Williams stated that the Fed's policy is currently moderately tight. Inflation will be "well above" 3% in the coming months. Uncertainty about the economic outlook limits the statements that Fed officials can make regarding the outlook for interest rate policy.

Williams said, "Given the numerous uncertainties in the current economic situation and the impact of the Middle East war, now is not the time to give clear guidance. If inflation falls back to 2%, I think lowering interest rates would be appropriate, but we are not at that stage yet." Goldman Sachs stated on April 16th that although it expects inflation to rise, economic growth to slow, and unemployment to rise slightly, the current shock is not enough to trigger a full-blown supply chain crisis, nor will it force the Federal Reserve to implement panic rate hikes. Goldman Sachs pointed out that the rise in unemployment, coupled with further improvement in core inflation, should be sufficient to offset the upward pressure from energy price transmission, which will provide strong support for rate cuts of 25 basis points in September and December respectively.

▌Cuban President Says “US Military Aggression Possible,” Urges Public to Prepare

On the 16th local time, Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel stated during an event in Havana that a “military aggression” by the United States against Cuba is possible. Díaz-Canel said that Cuba is experiencing an “absolutely challenging” moment, and the country faces an imminent “serious threat,” including “military aggression” from the United States. Díaz-Canel urged Cubans to be “prepared” to fight in the face of invasion. He said, “We do not want war, but we have a responsibility to be prepared.” Furthermore, Díaz-Canel condemned the “genocidal multi-layered blockade” imposed by the United States on Cuba, calling it the “main cause” of shortages and suffering in Cuban families, severely impacting the supply of basic necessities and development needs of the Cuban people.

(CCTV) ▌Iranian Armed Forces: Iranian Army is Fully Prepared for Defense On April 16 local time, Pakistani Army Chief of Staff Munir, during a meeting with Abdullah, Commander of the Hatem Anbia Central Command of the Iranian Armed Forces, briefed Pakistan on the measures taken to end the conflict and emphasized that these efforts would continue. Abdullah stressed, "If the enemy harbors malicious intent, the Iranian army is fully prepared for defense." (CCTV) ▌Federal Reserve's Milan: Only Three Rate Cuts Likely for the Remaining Year Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that, currently, it appears that only three rate cuts are likely for the remaining year.

▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in April is 99.5%

On April 17th, according to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Fed raising interest rates by 25 basis points in April is 0.5%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 99.5%. The probability of the Fed cumulatively cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by June is 1.4%, the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 98%, and the probability of cumulatively raising interest rates by 25 basis points is 0.5%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin still be called "digital gold" under geopolitical crises?

Every time there is a geopolitical crisis, gold prices rise, and Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been confirmed by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves.

For investors, Bitcoin exhibits an asymmetry: it falls with stocks but doesn't rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an overabundance of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," capable of functioning when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold, but rather a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.
